description_long: This object is a rectangular, wall-mounted traffic light with a weathered, dark metallic housing. The front face features two circular signal lights arranged vertically, with the upper light illuminated in red and the lower light currently off or very dim. The housing has a slightly rusted, aged appearance, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The signal lights are recessed within cylindrical hoods that protrude from the front, helping to shield the lenses from sunlight and rain. The back and sides of the housing are flat and unadorned, with no visible mounting hardware or control boxes. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of urban intersections or roadside installations.
description: A weathered, rectangular traffic light with two vertically stacked signal lights in a dark, rusted metal housing. The upper light is red and illuminated, while the lower light is off.
description_view_0: Front view showing both circular signal lights, with the upper red light illuminated and the lower light off, set in a dark, rusted housing.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the cylindrical hoods protruding from the housing, emphasizing the depth of the signal lights.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a flat, unadorned back panel with no visible features or mounting hardware.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the protruding cylindrical hoods and the thickness of the housing.
